mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, driving corporate earnings expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between RSAFS growth and forward 20-day SPX returns is negative or insignificant (|r|<0.1) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.5% and exhibits a rising trend over 3 months, monetary tightening depresses equity valuations via higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1.5% and rising by at least 25bp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: In instances where FEDFUNDS > 1.5% and rising, the average 60-day forward SPX return is not negative or the t-test fails to reject zero at 10% significance over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts (HOUST) and industrial production (INDPRO) signal coordinated weakening in both consumption and investment, raising recession probability and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months and INDPRO declines for 1 month
failure_condition: Following trigger events, the median 60-day forward SPX return is positive or the proportion of negative returns is less than 55% over any 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE below 4.5%), further declines in the unemployment rate indicate an overheating labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.0%, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not statistically positive (p<0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent upward trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns over the near term.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month change versus forward 20-day SPX return is less than 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) increases by more than 150,000 in a month, it indicates strong job creation that fuels income growth and consumer confidence, generating short-term positive momentum in equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: After payroll surprise > 150k, the 5-day forward SPX return is negative in at least 40% of cases, or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) accompanied by rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates a stagflationary environment where both inflation and real rates compress equity valuations via multiple compression and margin squeeze, pushing S&P 500 lower over a quarter.
failure_condition: When both CPIAUCSL > 250 and FEDFUNDS > 1.5%, the 60-day forward SPX return is positive more often than negative, or the average return is not statistically different from zero (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, positive real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals sustained economic expansion and improved corporate profitability, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: In quarters following a recession trough, if GDPC1 growth is positive, the 60-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 40% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly positive (p>0.10).
